_meta.description: Scientific targets and weights for all 25 CORTEX++ review axes
weight_principles: Climate/CO2 and basic needs (water, food) highest — direct civilizational threats
Human dignity axes (poverty, refugees, health) next tier
Governance, education, technology lower — important but derivative
Cosmos/long-term lowest — critical but further horizon
Technology trends (GitHub) minimal weight — proxy, not goal

CLIMATE_GLOBAL_RISK_REVIEW.rationale: IPCC safe CO2 ceiling (pre-industrial ~280, safe boundary ~350)
CLIMATE_GLOBAL_RISK_REVIEW.verification_fix: Manual check: Rockström et al. 2009, Nature 461:472. DOI:10.1038/461472a

ECOSYSTEMS_BIODIVERSITY_REVIEW.rationale: Planetary boundary for forest cover; current ~31% is below safe threshold
ECOSYSTEMS_BIODIVERSITY_REVIEW.verification_fix: Option A: Change metric to 'forest_remaining_pct_of_original' with biome-specific targets from Steffen et al. 2015. Option B: Keep 35% target but correct rationale to: 'Aspirational restoration target above current ~31% global forest cover (FAO Global Forest Resources Assessment 2020); inspired by P

ENERGY_REVIEW.rationale: IEA Net Zero 2050 requires 80%+ renewables by 2050
ENERGY_REVIEW.verification_fix: Clarify metric definition and update accordingly: If renewable_energy_pct = share of electricity generation → target_value: 90 (IEA NZE: 'almost 90% of electricity from renewables by 2050'). If renewable_energy_pct = share of total final energy → target_value: 67 (IEA NZE: 'two-thirds of total energ

FOOD_REVIEW.rationale: SDG2: near-zero hunger; current ~10% severely food insecure
FOOD_REVIEW.verification_fix: The 2.5% figure has a defensible interpretation: FAO defines <2.5% undernourishment as 'negligible/very low prevalence' threshold. If that is the intent, update rationale to: 'FAO defines <2.5% undernourishment as negligible; current global rate ~9.1% (SOFI 2023). SDG2 target is zero hunger.' Also u

MATERIALS_WASTE_REVIEW.rationale: Atmospheric CO2 as proxy for material throughput sustainability

WATER_REVIEW.rationale: SDG6: universal safe water access; current ~74%

COGNITION_LEARNING_REVIEW.rationale: Universal literacy as foundation for human autonomy (SDG4)

CULTURE_MEDIA_REVIEW.rationale: No single numeric threshold; LLM-assessed cultural health

GOVERNANCE_RIGHTS_HUMAN_REVIEW.rationale: Human rights protection; composite of democracy and rights indices

HUMAN_WELL_BEING_REVIEW.verification_fix: Consider increasing reference_worst from 100 to 200 to better reflect actual historical child mortality baselines (pre-1900). If 100 is kept as a 'worst modern scenario' (not historical), update rationale: 'reference_worst=100: worst plausible modern scenario (historical pre-industrial rates were 20

SOCIAL_RELATIONS_REVIEW.rationale: Pre-2011 baseline ~10M; reference_worst=120M (UNHCR 2023 peak: ~117M forcibly displaced globally)

ECONOMY_WORK_REVIEW.rationale: Resource-based model de-emphasizes GDP; stable sustainable activity preferred

GOVERNANCE_INSTITUTIONS_REVIEW.rationale: Institutional transparency and rule of law — no single global metric

INEQUALITY_POVERTY_REVIEW.rationale: SDG1: end extreme poverty; reference_worst=60% (World Bank est. ~60% poverty rate pre-1980 at $2.15/day threshold)

INFRASTRUCTURE_CITIES_REVIEW.rationale: Urbanization itself not a goal; sustainable city design matters more

TECHNOLOGY_AI_REVIEW.rationale: Beneficial AI adoption; no numeric global threshold exists

DEEP_TIME_RISKS_REVIEW.rationale: Asteroid/supervolcano risk — no actionable threshold at current tech level

GOAL_PROGRESS_REVIEW.rationale: Overall civilization goal progress — central meta-metric

LONG_TERM_FUTURE_REVIEW.rationale: Existential risk (nuclear, AGI misalignment, bio) — no single threshold

SPACE_INFRASTRUCTURE_REVIEW.rationale: Space redundancy goal — critical long-term, not short-term
